+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{remark}[Relation to the full theory]
 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category
@@ -300,8 +363,11 @@

-(Theorem~\ref{thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion}).

+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion}\textup{)}

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{remark}[Relation to the full theory]
 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

- $E_1$ pages on and off the Koszul locus.
+ The coderived bar-degree spectral sequence
+ \textup{(}Proposition~\ref{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}\textup{)}
+ has the same strict associated-graded $E_1$ page on and off the
+ Koszul locus; the difference is whether that page controls ordinary
+ cohomology or coderived coacyclicity.
 \item \emph{Completed Yangian case.}
  Corollary~\ref{cor:completed-bar-cobar-yangian}.
 \end{enumerate}

 eigenspace decomposition is well-defined.
 \end{proof}
 
+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 The provisional coderived category

 \end{proof}
 
+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{remark}[Relation to the full theory]
 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category
@@ -300,8 +363,11 @@

 and curved $A_\infty$-coalgebras without filtrations. The remaining
-gap is off-Koszul bar-cobar inversion
-(Theorem~\ref{thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion}).
+gap is not coderived inversion itself

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

\index{bar-cobar adjunction!coderived formulation}
In the setting of this monograph, the bar complex
$\bar{B}^{(g)}(\cA)$ is a chiral CDG-coalgebra with curvature
$m_0^{(g)} = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \omega_g$
(Convention~\ref{conv:higher-genus-differentials}).
At genus~$0$, $m_0^{(0)} = 0$ and
Theorem~\ref{thm:conilpotent-reduction} applies: the coderived
category is the ordinary derived category, and bar-cobar
inversion on the strict Koszul lane
\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}
is a statement in $D(\bar{B}^{(0)}\text{-}\mathrm{comod})$.
At genus $g \geq 1$ with $\kappa(\cA) \neq 0$, the bar complex
is genuinely curved, and the underlying bar complex is acyclic on
the ordinary cochain-complex surface
\textup{(}Proposition~\ref{prop:curved-bar-acyclicity}\textup{)}.
Bar-cobar inversion therefore becomes a statement in the
coderived category
$D^{\mathrm{co}}(\bar{B}^{(g)}\text{-}\mathrm{comod})$.
The Koszul duality equivalence
$D^{\mathrm{co}}(\barB^{(g)}(\cA)\text{-}\mathrm{comod})
 succeeded in 51ms:

isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
in the essential image of~$\iota$.
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.

the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on

$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
strict complexes.

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{From adjunction to inversion.}\enspace
An adjunction that is not an equivalence is a deficiency: it
records the information lost in the round trip. Theorem~A
gives the adjunction $\Omegach \dashv \barBch$; the question is
whether the counit $\psi \colon \Omegach(\barBch(\cA)) \to \cA$
is a quasi-isomorphism, so that no information is lost. This is
not a formal consequence of the adjunction; it requires the
geometry of the Fulton--MacPherson compactification and the
Koszulness of the underlying chiral operad.

Theorem~B (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}) gives the
answer in two layers: on the Koszul locus
(Definition~\ref{def:koszul-locus}), the counit~$\psi$ is a
quasi-isomorphism of chiral algebras, genus by genus and in
the $\hbar$-adic completion
(Theorem~\ref{thm:genus-graded-convergence}); off the Koszul locus,
the same counit remains invertible in the coderived
bar-coalgebra category, and promotion back to ordinary chain
cohomology requires an extra collapse input. The strict proof uses
 succeeded in 52ms:
The proof assembles the chain-level co-contra correspondence
(Theorem~\ref{thm:chiral-co-contra-correspondence}) across
strata using the factorization structure.

\emph{Step~1: Stratum-by-stratum equivalence.}
On each stratum $\operatorname{Ran}_n(X)$, the restriction
$C_n = C|_{\operatorname{Ran}_n}$ is a conilpotent CDG-coalgebra
with finite-dimensional graded pieces (finite-dimensionality
propagates from the $1$-point stratum via the factorization
tensor product: near the generic point of $\operatorname{Ran}_n$,
$C_n \simeq C_1^{\otimes n}$, which is finite-dimensional in each
graded piece if $C_1$ is).
Theorem~\ref{thm:chiral-co-contra-correspondence} gives
equivalences
\begin{equation}\label{eq:stratum-co-contra}
D^{\mathrm{co}}(C_n\text{-}\mathrm{comod}^{\mathrm{ch}})
\simeq
D^{\mathrm{ctr}}(C_n\text{-}\mathrm{contra}^{\mathrm{ch}}),
\end{equation}
implemented by the Positselski functors $\Phi_{C_n}$ and
 succeeded in 51ms:

\begin{theorem}[Bar-cobar inversion: strict on the Koszul locus, coderived off it; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}
\textup{[Regime: quadratic at genus~$0$; curved-central at genus~$g \geq 1$
on the Koszul locus
\textup{(}cf.\ Convention~\textup{\ref{conv:regime-tags})}.]}

The Heisenberg inversion $\Omega^{\mathrm{ch}}(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\mathcal{H}_k)) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{H}_k$ (\S\ref{sec:frame-inversion}) generalizes to all Koszul chiral algebras.

Let $\mathcal{A}$ be a complete augmented chiral algebra on a
Riemann surface~$X$, with
$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\mathcal{A})$ conilpotent or $\mathcal{A}$
complete with respect to its augmentation ideal
\textup{(}\S\ref{sec:i-adic-completion}\textup{)}, and with
finite-dimensional graded bar pieces whenever the coderived clause
below is invoked. The bar-cobar counit
\[
\psi \colon \Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})) \longrightarrow \mathcal{A}
\]
is the adjunction counit and hence a canonical morphism of chiral
algebras. Its strongest proved form is:
 succeeded in 52ms:

\emph{Step 4: Higher differentials.} For $r \geq 2$:
\[d_r: E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}\]

These differentials encode higher-order interactions between bar and cobar operations.

\emph{Step 5: Convergence.} The spectral sequence converges because:
For the application to Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}, the completeness statement in Definition~\ref{def:bar-cobar-filtration}(2) is supplied by its conilpotency/completion hypothesis: if $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is conilpotent in the sense of Definition~\ref{def:conilpotent-complete}, every element has finite bar degree, while in the augmentation-ideal completion regime the filtration is complete by construction.
\begin{enumerate}
\item The filtration is complete: $\bigcap_p F^p = 0$
\item The filtration is exhaustive: $\bigcup_p F^p = \Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{A}))$
\item The complex is bounded in each column (fixed $p$)
\end{enumerate}

By standard spectral sequence theory (Weibel \cite{Weibel94}, Chapter 5), this ensures:
\[E_\infty^{p,q} = \text{Gr}^p H^{p+q}(\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})))\]
\end{proof}

\textup{[Regime: quadratic, Koszul locus, square-zero total differential

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

+the filtered cone vanishes. By

+curved object with acyclic associated graded is coacyclic.

+totalizations of short exact sequences and infinite direct sums, so
+$\varepsilon$ is a coacyclic-equivalence and hence an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$.

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

-(Theorem~\ref{thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion}).

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '2146,2178p' chapters/theory/bar_cobar_adjunction_inversion.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 51ms:
\begin{proof}
The Koszul property of $\mathcal{A}$ means that the natural inclusion $\iota: \mathcal{A}^! \hookrightarrow \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ of the quadratic dual into the bar complex is a quasi-isomorphism (see \cite[Theorem~3.4.1]{LV12} for the classical case and Chapter~\ref{chap:koszul-pairs} for the chiral adaptation). Since $\mathcal{A}^!$ is generated in weight~1 with quadratic relations, its image under $\iota$ lies on the diagonal $p + q = \text{const}$ in the bigrading $(p,q)$ by bar degree and internal degree.

At the $E_1$ page, we compute the cohomology of each column with respect to $d_0 = d_{\bar{B}}$, the full bar complex differential. Each column $E_0^{p,\bullet}$ with differential $d_0$ computes the bar cohomology $H^q(\bar{B}^p(\mathcal{A}), d_{\bar{B}})$ at fixed cobar degree~$p$. By the Koszul quasi-isomorphism, the bar cohomology is concentrated on the diagonal, giving $E_1^{p,q} = 0$ whenever $q \neq 0$ (after reindexing by the bar filtration degree).

At the $E_2$ page, we take cohomology with respect to $d_1$ (induced by $d_{\text{cobar}}$, the comultiplication part). The $E_2$ groups inherit the diagonal concentration.

For $r \geq 2$, the differential $d_r: E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r, q-r+1}$ shifts the bidegree by $(r, 1-r)$. Since $E_2^{p,q} = 0$ for $q \neq 0$, either the source or the target of $d_r$ vanishes for every $r \geq 2$. Therefore $d_r = 0$ for all $r \geq 2$, and the spectral sequence collapses at $E_2$.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Square-zero boundary of the spectral-sequence proof]
\label{rem:spectral-sequence-square-zero-boundary}
Theorems~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-spectral-sequence}
and~\ref{thm:spectral-sequence-collapse} are statements about filtered
complexes with square-zero total differential. In this chapter they
apply to the strict genus-$0$ bar-cobar complex, and to any
completed/corrected model whose total differential squares to zero.
They do \emph{not} apply to the curved fiberwise higher-genus
differential $\dfib$ with
$\dfib^{\,2} = \kappa(\cA)\cdot\omega_g$. Off the Koszul locus, the
file update:

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

\textup{[Regime: curved-central on the Koszul locus
\textup{(}Convention~\textup{\ref{conv:regime-tags})}.]}

Let $\mathcal{A}$ be a Koszul chiral algebra satisfying the hypotheses of
Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}. The bar-cobar inversion
$\psi: \Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})) \to \mathcal{A}$
converges at each genus $g$, and the full genus-graded sum converges in the
$\hbar$-adic completion.

More precisely:
\begin{enumerate}
\item \emph{Genus zero:} 
 \[\psi_0: \Omega_0(\bar{B}_0(\mathcal{A})) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{A}\]
 is a quasi-isomorphism (classical result, BD §3.7)
 
\item \emph{Fixed genus $g$:}
 \[\psi_g: \Omega_g(\bar{B}_g(\mathcal{A})) \to \mathcal{A}_g\]
 is a quasi-isomorphism after appropriate quantum corrections (here $\mathcal{A}_g$ denotes the genus-$g$ component of the genus-graded chiral algebra)
 
\item \emph{Genus series:}

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

+proved replacement is the coderived bar-degree filtration of
+Proposition~\ref{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence},
+together with the intrinsic coderived/contraderived bar-coalgebra
+comparison of Theorem~\ref{thm:positselski-chiral-proved}. Ordinary
+derived reduction remains available only on the flat finite-type
+completed-dual loci of Theorem~\ref{thm:full-derived-module-equiv-proved}
+or when the curvature collapses.
 \end{remark}

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{remark}[Relation to the full theory]
 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

\Omega_X \barB_X(\cA) \longrightarrow \cA
\]
is an isomorphism in
$D^{\mathrm{co}}(\barB\text{-}\mathrm{CoFact})$,
without any Koszulness hypothesis on~$\cA$.
Equivalently, its cone is coacyclic.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
Write $C = \barB_X^{\mathrm{fact}}(\cA)$ for the bar coalgebra
(a conilpotent curved factorization coalgebra by
Proposition~\ref{prop:bar-ran-well-defined}),
and $\varepsilon \colon \Omega_X C \to \cA$ for the bar-cobar
counit. Set
$K := \operatorname{cone}(\varepsilon)$.
We show $K$ is coacyclic in
$D^{\mathrm{co}}(C\text{-}\mathrm{CoFact})$ by the same three-step
assembly used in the proof of
Theorem~\ref{thm:fact-co-contra-general}.

 equivalently, it is the implication

+Under the higher-genus hypotheses of

+and Theorem~\ref{thm:genus-graded-convergence} supplies the completed

+
+\item \emph{Coderived off-Koszul lane.}
+Theorem~\ref{thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion} proves that the
+factorization counit $\psi_X$ is an isomorphism in the coderived
+category for every complete augmented input with finite-dimensional
+graded bar pieces. Its proof is independent of
+Theorem~\ref{thm:higher-genus-inversion}: it identifies the cone of
+$\psi_X$ as coacyclic by stratified Positselski inversion and the
+Verdier-quotient criterion for
+$D^{\mathrm{co}}(\barB\text{-}\mathrm{CoFact})$.

+\item \emph{Coderived bar-degree filtration.}

+Theorems~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-spectral-sequence}
+and~\ref{thm:spectral-sequence-collapse}. On the curved surface,
+Proposition~\ref{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}

+associated-graded $E_1$ page is strict because the curvature term lies
+in positive filtration.
 
-\item \emph{Spectral sequence statement.}

+If $\kappa(\mathcal{A}) = 0$, then the curved differential becomes
+square-zero, so Theorem~\ref{thm:conilpotent-reduction} identifies the
+coderived and ordinary derived categories and the coderived
+equivalence of~(D2) is an ordinary quasi-isomorphism. More generally,
+any class~$G/L$ collapse input for the coderived bar-degree spectral
+sequence turns coacyclicity of the cone into acyclicity, hence into an
+ordinary quasi-isomorphism. This is the exact extra hypothesis isolated
+in clause~(4).
 \end{enumerate}
-Combining (D1)--(D4) gives the genus-$0$ clause unconditionally and the
-higher-genus clauses under axiom~\ref{MK:modular}.
+Combining (D1)--(D4) gives the strongest proved package stated in the
+theorem.
 \end{proof}

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

thinking
**Identifying potential issues in theorem patches**

There's a stray closing brace at the end of the sentence after the "no Koszulness hypothesis," and I'll need to fix that. I should also consider removing the extra brace from `\emph{Equivalently,}` and combine it into the sentence. 

Additionally, the proof for Theorem\ref{thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion} can be simplified. There are some potential issues to check regarding other theorems and statements, especially about ambiguous phrasing and accuracy. I plan to address these by making necessary patches.
thinking
**Addressing potential theorem issues**

I’m noticing a potential issue where the `Scope` section states that there's no qi from the square-zero collapse argument. Then, it mentions, "this theorem asserts the coderived statement of (2), and isolates in (4)," which seems fine. 

I should patch the `rem:completed-ambient-status` since it hasn't been reviewed yet but is likely okay. I also want to check for any AP29 issues later. Overall, I'll focus on fixing these small issues as I go.
codex
I found one concrete syntax issue from the reread: a stray brace in the off-Koszul theorem proof. I’m fixing that, tightening the regime line, and disambiguating the convergence theorem so it points only to the strict/Koszul clause of Theorem B.

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{remark}[Relation to the full theory]
 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

+$\varepsilon$ is a coacyclic-equivalence and hence an isomorphism in

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{remark}[Relation to the full theory]
 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category
@@ -300,8 +363,11 @@

 handles pro-nilpotent completions, infinitely generated bar complexes,
 and curved $A_\infty$-coalgebras without filtrations. The remaining
-gap is off-Koszul bar-cobar inversion
-(Theorem~\ref{thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion}).
+gap is not coderived inversion itself
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion}\textup{)}
+but its promotion back to an ordinary chain-level
+quasi-isomorphism away from the loci where the coderived
+bar-degree spectral sequence collapses.
 \end{remark}

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 The provisional coderived category

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '214,236p' chapters/theory/bar_cobar_adjunction_inversion.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobarexec
/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n \"higher-genus-inversion|bar-cobar-inversion-qi\\}\\([0-9]\" chapters/theory/coderived_models.tex chapters/theory/bar_cobar_adjunction_inversion.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 51ms:
\[W(z)W(w) = \frac{\cdots}{(z-w)^6} + \cdots + \frac{\Lambda(L \cdot L)(w)}{(z-w)^2} + \cdots\]
where $\Lambda(L \cdot L)$ is a composite operator, not a single generator. The algebra is filtered rather than curved because it is not generated by a finite-dimensional space $V$: composite operators like $(L \cdot L)$ appear at all levels, and the filtration is infinite-dimensional at each level. The Koszul dual is
\[W_3^! = \widehat{\mathrm{CoW}_3}
\quad \text{(completed cooperad structure)}.\]
The bar construction must be completed:
\[\widehat{\bar{B}}(W_3) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(W_3)/F_n\]
where $F_n$ is the filtration by operator dimension.
\end{example}

\subsubsection{Class IV: general (infinite-type completion)}

\begin{example}[\texorpdfstring{$W_\infty$}{W_infinity}: infinite-type completion]\label{ex:winfty-completion-frontier-comprehensive}
The $W_\infty$ algebra has:
\begin{itemize}
\item Generators: $W^{(n)}$ for all $n \geq 2$ (infinitely many generators)
\item Relations: Infinitely many non-linear relations
\item No finite presentation
\end{itemize}

\begin{remark}[Closure of structural gap~H1]
\label{rem:H1-closure}
\index{structural gaps!H1 closed}
Theorem~\ref{thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion} resolves
structural gap~H1 (Remark~\ref{rem:structural-gaps}):
the coderived Ran-space formalism is now established.
Combined with the strict Koszul-locus inversion of
Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}, this gives the strongest
proved package on the present surface: ordinary quasi-isomorphism on
the Koszul lane, coderived equivalence for all complete augmented
chiral algebras with finite-dimensional bar complex, and no silent
promotion of the off-Koszul statement back to ordinary cohomology.
The remaining issue is exactly the collapse input needed for that
promotion.

The three-step proof (stratum-by-stratum Positselski,
factorization compatibility, conservative assembly) is the
factorization incarnation of Positselski's abstract
programme. The only input beyond the abstract theory is
the CDG-factorization compatibility
 succeeded in 52ms:
$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
acyclicity of the associated graded cone
$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
strict complexes.

Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Relation to the full theory]
\label{rem:coderived-full}
The provisional coderived category
$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$ embeds fully faithfully
into the coderived category of curved factorization coalgebras on

+ \textup{(}Definition~\ref{def:koszul-chiral-algebra}; equivalently,
+ $\cA \in \operatorname{Kosz}(X)$\textup{)}, then the genus-$0$
+ counit
+ \[
+ \psi_0 \colon \Omega_0(\bar{B}_0(\mathcal{A})) \longrightarrow \mathcal{A}
+ \]
+ is a quasi-isomorphism of chiral algebras.
+ If, in addition, $\mathcal{A}$ lies on the higher-genus Koszul locus
+ of Theorem~\ref{thm:higher-genus-inversion}
+ \textup{(}for the standard landscape, by
+ Theorems~\ref{thm:pbw-allgenera-km}--\ref{thm:pbw-allgenera-principal-w}\textup{)},
+ then each genus component
+ \[
+ \psi_g \colon \Omega_g(\bar{B}_g(\mathcal{A})) \longrightarrow \mathcal{A}_g
+ \]
+ and the completed series
+ \[
+ \psi = \sum_{g=0}^\infty \hbar^{2g-2}\psi_g
+ \]
+ are quasi-isomorphisms.
 
-\item For each genus $g \geq 1$, the genus-$g$ component:
- \[\psi_g: \Omega_g(\bar{B}_g(\mathcal{A})) \longrightarrow \mathcal{A}_g\]
- is a quasi-isomorphism
+\item \emph{Coderived off-Koszul lane.}
+ For arbitrary complete augmented $\mathcal{A}$ with
+ finite-dimensional graded bar pieces, the factorization counit
+ \[
+ \psi_X \colon \Omega_X \barB_X(\mathcal{A}) \longrightarrow \mathcal{A}
+ \]
+ is a coacyclic-equivalence: its cone
+ \[
+ K_\psi :=
+ \operatorname{cone}\!\bigl(\Omega_X \barB_X(\mathcal{A}) \to \mathcal{A}\bigr)
+ \]
+ is coacyclic. Equivalently, $\psi_X$ is an isomorphism in
+ $D^{\mathrm{co}}(\barB\text{-}\mathrm{CoFact})$, without any
+ Koszulness hypothesis.
 
-\item The full genus-graded map:
- \[\psi = \sum_{g=0}^\infty \hbar^{2g-2}\psi_g: \Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})) \longrightarrow \mathcal{A}\]

+\item \emph{Coderived bar-degree filtration.}
+ On the curved cone $K_\psi$, the bar-degree filtration yields the
+ coderived spectral sequence of
+ Proposition~\ref{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}. Its
+ $E_1$ page is computed from the strict associated-graded differential
+ $\operatorname{gr}^0 d$; the curvature relation
+ $\dfib^{\,2} = \kappa(\mathcal{A}) \cdot \omega_g$ is absorbed into
+ positive filtration and does not obstruct the $E_1$ page.
+ On the square-zero Koszul lane this reduces to
+ Theorems~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-spectral-sequence}
+ and~\ref{thm:spectral-sequence-collapse}.
 
-\item There exists a spectral sequence converging to
- $H^\bullet(\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})))$
- with $E_1$-page given by the bar-cobar complex; it collapses at $E_2$
- by the Koszul property (Theorem~\ref{thm:spectral-sequence-collapse})
+\item \emph{Promotion back to ordinary quasi-isomorphism.}
+ If $\kappa(\mathcal{A}) = 0$, or more generally if the coderived
+ bar-degree spectral sequence degenerates to ordinary cohomology on
+ the relevant off-Koszul locus
+ \textup{(}the class~$G/L$ collapse input\textup{)}, then the
+ coderived equivalence of~(2) upgrades to a chain-level
+ quasi-isomorphism. Outside those collapse loci this theorem does not
+ assert an ordinary quasi-isomorphism.
 \end{enumerate}

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

+$\varepsilon$ is a coacyclic-equivalence and hence an isomorphism in

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '108,122p' Makefile" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
	@echo "     Logs: $(LOG_DIR)/tex-build.stdout.log and $(MAIN).log"
	@echo ""

## fast: Bounded quick build for rapid iteration.
##   Runs enough passes to settle references in normal editing flows, while
##   still capping the work below the full build target.
fast:
	@echo "  ── Fast build (up to $(FAST_PASSES) passes) ──"
	@mkdir -p $(LOG_DIR)
	@$(BUILD_SCRIPT) $(FAST_PASSES)
	@echo "     Logs: $(LOG_DIR)/tex-build.stdout.log and $(MAIN).log"

## working-notes: Build the working notes (standalone document).
working-notes: $(OUT_WN)

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

The AP greps are clean for the two mathematical red flags we were targeting: no bare `\Omega/z` hits and no bar-augmentation/suspension regressions in the edited files. The slop grep only surfaced pre-existing `we now`/`standard landscape` occurrences, so I’m moving to the build surface to check that the theorem/proposition rewrites still compile and reference cleanly.

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 The provisional coderived category
