+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

+ Koszul locus; the difference is whether that page controls ordinary
+ cohomology or coderived coacyclicity.
 \item \emph{Completed Yangian case.}
  Corollary~\ref{cor:completed-bar-cobar-yangian}.
 \end{enumerate}

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11}, a bounded-below filtered

+$\varepsilon$ is a coacyclic-equivalence and hence an isomorphism in

 eigenspace decomposition is well-defined.
 \end{proof}
 
+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 \label{rem:coderived-full}

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

**Considering build issues**

I’m wondering if the undefined references skyrocketing might be related to a build issue. It could be worth taking a step back to check if anything changed in the build process or compile settings. I might need to wait before drawing conclusions. There could be more to it, or maybe it's a simple fix! I'll keep an eye on this and follow up once I gather more information.

+\item \emph{Strict Koszul lane.}

 equivalently, it is the implication

-Under axiom~\ref{MK:modular}, the genus-$g$ components $\psi_g$ for

+Under the higher-genus hypotheses of
+Theorem~\ref{thm:higher-genus-inversion}, the genus-$g$ components

+and Theorem~\ref{thm:genus-graded-convergence} supplies the completed
+series statement.
 
-\item \emph{Full genus-graded convergence and quasi-isomorphism.}
-Under the same higher-genus hypothesis, this is
-Theorem~\ref{thm:genus-graded-convergence}, item~(3), which identifies
-the completed genus series $\psi=\sum_{g\ge 0}\hbar^{2g-2}\psi_g$ and
-proves convergence.
+\item \emph{Coderived off-Koszul lane.}
+Theorem~\ref{thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion} proves that the
+factorization counit $\psi_X$ is an isomorphism in the coderived
+category for every complete augmented input with finite-dimensional
+graded bar pieces. Its proof is independent of
+Theorem~\ref{thm:higher-genus-inversion}: it identifies the cone of
+$\psi_X$ as coacyclic by stratified Positselski inversion and the
+Verdier-quotient criterion for
+$D^{\mathrm{co}}(\barB\text{-}\mathrm{CoFact})$.
+
+\item \emph{Coderived bar-degree filtration.}
+On the strict square-zero surface, existence and collapse are exactly
+Theorems~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-spectral-sequence}
+and~\ref{thm:spectral-sequence-collapse}. On the curved surface,
+Proposition~\ref{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+replaces ordinary abutment by coacyclicity of the cone: the
+associated-graded $E_1$ page is strict because the curvature term lies
+in positive filtration.
 
-\item \emph{Spectral sequence statement.}
-Existence and explicit page description are exactly
-Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-spectral-sequence}; collapse for Koszul input is
-Theorem~\ref{thm:spectral-sequence-collapse}.
+\item \emph{Promotion lane.}
+If $\kappa(\mathcal{A}) = 0$, then the curved differential becomes
+square-zero, so Theorem~\ref{thm:conilpotent-reduction} identifies the
+coderived and ordinary derived categories and the coderived
+equivalence of~(D2) is an ordinary quasi-isomorphism. More generally,
+any class~$G/L$ collapse input for the coderived bar-degree spectral
+sequence turns coacyclicity of the cone into acyclicity, hence into an
+ordinary quasi-isomorphism. This is the exact extra hypothesis isolated
+in clause~(4).
 \end{enumerate}
-Combining (D1)--(D4) gives the genus-$0$ clause unconditionally and the
-higher-genus clauses under axiom~\ref{MK:modular}.
+Combining (D1)--(D4) gives the strongest proved package stated in the
+theorem.
 \end{proof}

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

+\item \emph{Coderived bar-degree filtration.}
+ On the curved cone $K_\psi$, the bar-degree filtration yields the
+ coderived spectral sequence of
+ Proposition~\ref{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}. Its

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

+$K|_{\operatorname{Ran}_n(X)}$ on each stratum~$n$ by Step~1.
+By the conservativity clause of the stratified conservative
+restriction theorem
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:stratified-conservative-restriction}\textup{)},
+$K$ is coacyclic in the factorization sense.
+Therefore $\varepsilon$ becomes an isomorphism in
+$D^{\mathrm{co}}(C\text{-}\mathrm{CoFact})$.
 \end{proof}

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{remark}[Relation to the full theory]
 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

**Inspecting labels in file**

I need to check if the label exists in the same file, and maybe there's an unresolved issue due to oscillation. It seems like I should inspect the `.aux` file. I wonder if making more passes could help resolve things? It would be good to search for `newlabel{thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion}` in the main `.aux` and also look for new property labels using the `rg` command. Let’s see what I find!
codex

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

+curvature term lies in positive filtration, so the
+associated-graded $E_1$ page is strict, while coacyclicity of the
+cone records the actual inversion statement. The regime hierarchy
+(Convention~\ref{conv:regime-tags}) organizes these

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 \end{proof}
 
+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived

thinking
**Investigating aux models**

I'm considering whether the derived models can create their own auxiliary files, but it seems they weren't generated. It might be a compile issue, as the main build includes pages up to 2610. That’s a bit strange. I think I should search for the label in "main.aux," but maybe it’s not there. I’ll look through all auxiliary files more directly for clearer results.

 eigenspace decomposition is well-defined.

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 The provisional coderived category

 \smallskip\noindent\emph{Scope.}

 quotients and minimal-model Virasoro/W-algebras the PBW/Shapovalov

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

**Investigating build issues**

I need to take a closer look around line 1480 to check for the absence of `\endinput`. It seems that the build script might be using a truncated version of `main_minimal.tex` instead of the expected `main.tex`, even though the output indicates it's building `main.tex`. Hm, that’s a little confusing. I think I should search the log file located in `.build_logs/tex-build.stdout.log` for references to coderived_models to clarify what’s going on.

I checked the aux surface. `thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi` is being written correctly, but the auxiliary files still aren’t recording the new coderived-model labels during this oscillating global build, which matches the undefined-reference noise for `thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion`. I’m checking the build script/log path next to see whether the fast build is truncating the document or just failing to settle those late-file labels.

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

- (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-spectral-sequence}) has matching
- $E_1$ pages on and off the Koszul locus.
+ The coderived bar-degree spectral sequence
+ \textup{(}Proposition~\ref{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}\textup{)}
+ has the same strict associated-graded $E_1$ page on and off the
+ Koszul locus; the difference is whether that page controls ordinary
+ cohomology or coderived coacyclicity.
 \item \emph{Completed Yangian case.}
  Corollary~\ref{cor:completed-bar-cobar-yangian}.
 \end{enumerate}

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' scripts/build.sh" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
#!/bin/bash
# Converging build script for the chiral bar-cobar manuscript.
# Runs up to MAX_PASSES of pdflatex, stopping when references stabilize.
# Use this instead of raw pdflatex to avoid oscillation on large documents.

set -euo pipefail
cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."

MAX_PASSES=${1:-7}
TEX="pdflatex"
TEXFLAGS="-interaction=batchmode -file-line-error -synctex=0 -cnf-line=buf_size=1000000 -cnf-line=stack_size=20000"
LOG_DIR=".build_logs"
RUN_LOG="$LOG_DIR/tex-build.stdout.log"
LOCK_DIR="$LOG_DIR/.build.lock"

mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"

acquire_lock() {
    local announced=0
    while ! mkdir "$LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null; do
file update:

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{remark}[Relation to the full theory]
 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

 \begin{remark}[Status]\label{rem:completed-ambient-status}

-monograph proves bar-cobar equivalence in the pro-nilpotent regime

+monograph proves ordinary bar-cobar quasi-isomorphism on the

thinking
**Investigating build logs**

I should search the build logs for our label names in `.build_logs/tex-build.stdout.log`, but it gets overwritten each time, so there might not be any mention of it. It’s not crucial, since we know the build script is complete. The `coderived_models.tex` is missing from the log, which might be due to pdflatex not echoing filenames in batch mode unless there are errors. I guess we can check the `main.log` for warnings around `thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion`. Running more passes might help settle things down, maybe 2 extra passes?
thinking
**Verifying local labels**

To verify local labels, I might find it easier to use a single `pdflatex` since the current auxiliary files are already updated. Maybe running one or two more `make fast` commands could help, though there are costs involved. I could also consider running `./scripts/build.sh 2` to settle things quickly. But if there are thousands of undefined references due to the repository, it may not be worth it. It's possible that specific undefined references for the new theorem are linked to the order of files, which might require extra passes to resolve.
thinking
**Examining auxiliary files**

It looks like the `bar_cobar_adjunction.aux` might exist due to a `\chapter` file, but I'm not sure. Could there be a custom package affecting this? The `main.aux` should ideally contain new labels from all files if the build reaches the end. My search through the auxiliary files shows none, which suggests that the labels weren't written because the build didn’t go that far. However, the build output pages up to 2610, so there might be later connections that aren't being captured.

+totalizations of short exact sequences and infinite direct sums, so
+$\varepsilon$ is a coacyclic-equivalence and hence an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$.

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 \label{rem:coderived-full}
 The provisional coderived category

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

**Troubleshooting LaTeX Compilation**

I’m dealing with an unresolved property in LaTeX. I think I might need to run pdflatex again after the main aux file, as the label could still not be written because of the later inputs. However, if the labels were written, that might resolve things. I’ll check all auxiliary files for labels. It’s possible that since `\input{chapters/theory/coderived_models}` is called after `\includeonly`, it wasn’t compiled due to that setting. I'll search through the configuration to be sure.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "includeonly" -S main.tex .' in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
./wave2_audit_20260413_001942/C20_e1_modular_koszul.md:664:I’m thinking about using pdflatex with a command like `-jobname` and `\input`, but the file has chapter environments and macros from the preamble, so it can’t stand alone. Maybe I could use `main.tex` with `\includeonly`, but if `main.tex` includes other chapters, that could still lead to compile failures. I might check for `\includeonly` in `main.tex`, though searching with `rg` should be useful. I need to look for various patterns and phrases to identify issues in the LaTeX file—there's a lot to consider here!

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

   418	The $\Eone$-chiral face of this ambient is established: the
   419	monograph proves ordinary bar-cobar quasi-isomorphism on the
   420	Koszul locus and coderived bar-cobar inversion for arbitrary
   421	complete augmented inputs with finite-dimensional bar pieces
   422	\textup{(}Theorems~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}
   423	and~\ref{thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion}\textup{)}. The full
   424	$\Einf$-factorization version belongs to the Stratum~II programme
   425	(Remark~\ref{rem:two-strata}). The homotopy form of the modular
   426	Koszul definition
   427	(Definition~\ref{def:modular-koszul-homotopy}) uses
   428	$\Alg_{\mathrm{aug}}$ rather than
   429	$\Alg_{\mathrm{aug,comp}}$ because all objects treated in this
   430	monograph are already complete (their bar complexes converge).
   431	\end{remark}

   156	Theorem~\ref{thm:conilpotent-reduction} applies: the coderived
   157	category is the ordinary derived category, and bar-cobar
   158	inversion on the strict Koszul lane

   160	is a statement in $D(\bar{B}^{(0)}\text{-}\mathrm{comod})$.
   161	At genus $g \geq 1$ with $\kappa(\cA) \neq 0$, the bar complex
   162	is genuinely curved, and the underlying bar complex is acyclic on
   163	the ordinary cochain-complex surface
   164	\textup{(}Proposition~\ref{prop:curved-bar-acyclicity}\textup{)}.
   165	Bar-cobar inversion therefore becomes a statement in the
   166	coderived category
   167	$D^{\mathrm{co}}(\bar{B}^{(g)}\text{-}\mathrm{comod})$.
   168	The Koszul duality equivalence
   169	$D^{\mathrm{co}}(\barB^{(g)}(\cA)\text{-}\mathrm{comod})
   170	\simeq D^{\mathrm{ctr}}(\barB^{(g)}(\cA)\text{-}\mathrm{contra})$
   171	\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:positselski-chiral-proved}\textup{)} is the chiral
   172	incarnation of the comodule-contramodule correspondence
   173	\eqref{eq:co-contra-equivalence}.

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

**Patching modified lines**

I see a few lines that might need some tweaking, especially around the AP29 hits. For instance, in line 1713 where it says "we now explain," that might not be necessary since it's preexisting in the modified region. I think it could help to patch things up by replacing "we now prove" and similar phrases with something cleaner like "The central result of this chapter is:" and "the concrete mechanism is as follows." This could really help reduce sloppiness in the text.
thinking
**Checking for more patches**

I'm contemplating whether there are additional instances of “we now” in the modified region, particularly around lines 1602 and 1713. It seems advisable to patch those. For the section stating “Theorem B gives the answer in two layers,” I think that's fine as is. Making these adjustments could really help reduce unnecessary grep hits and clean up the text overall. It’s all about tightening up the language here.

+Under the higher-genus hypotheses of

+
+\item \emph{Coderived off-Koszul lane.}
+Theorem~\ref{thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion} proves that the
+factorization counit $\psi_X$ is an isomorphism in the coderived
+category for every complete augmented input with finite-dimensional
+graded bar pieces. Its proof is independent of
+Theorem~\ref{thm:higher-genus-inversion}: it identifies the cone of
+$\psi_X$ as coacyclic by stratified Positselski inversion and the
+Verdier-quotient criterion for
+$D^{\mathrm{co}}(\barB\text{-}\mathrm{CoFact})$.

+\item \emph{Coderived bar-degree filtration.}
+On the strict square-zero surface, existence and collapse are exactly
+Theorems~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-spectral-sequence}
+and~\ref{thm:spectral-sequence-collapse}. On the curved surface,
+Proposition~\ref{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}

+associated-graded $E_1$ page is strict because the curvature term lies
+in positive filtration.
 
-\item \emph{Spectral sequence statement.}

+\item \emph{Promotion lane.}
+If $\kappa(\mathcal{A}) = 0$, then the curved differential becomes
+square-zero, so Theorem~\ref{thm:conilpotent-reduction} identifies the
+coderived and ordinary derived categories and the coderived
+equivalence of~(D2) is an ordinary quasi-isomorphism. More generally,
+any class~$G/L$ collapse input for the coderived bar-degree spectral
+sequence turns coacyclicity of the cone into acyclicity, hence into an
+ordinary quasi-isomorphism. This is the exact extra hypothesis isolated
+in clause~(4).
 \end{enumerate}

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

 The provisional coderived category

 \subsection{Statement of the main result}
 
-\begin{theorem}[Bar-cobar inversion is quasi-isomorphism; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}
-\textup{[Regime: quadratic at genus~$0$; curved-central at genus~$g \geq 1$
-on the Koszul locus
+\begin{theorem}[Bar-cobar inversion: strict on the Koszul locus, coderived off it; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}
+\textup{[Regime: quadratic on the strict lane; curved-central and
+filtered-complete on the coderived lane
 \textup{(}cf.\ Convention~\textup{\ref{conv:regime-tags})}.]}
 
 The Heisenberg inversion $\Omega^{\mathrm{ch}}(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\mathcal{H}_k)) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{H}_k$ (\S\ref{sec:frame-inversion}) generalizes to all Koszul chiral algebras.
 
-Let $\mathcal{A}$ be a \emph{Koszul} chiral algebra on a Riemann surface $X$
-(Definition~\ref{def:koszul-chiral-algebra};
-equivalently, $\cA \in \operatorname{Kosz}(X)$;
-this excludes simple admissible-level quotients and minimal-model
-central charges; see Scope below), with
-$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\mathcal{A})$ conilpotent or $\mathcal{A}$ complete
-with respect to its augmentation ideal
-\textup{(}\S\ref{sec:i-adic-completion}\textup{)}. Then the genus-$0$
-counit
+Let $\mathcal{A}$ be a complete augmented chiral algebra on a
+Riemann surface~$X$, with
+$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\mathcal{A})$ conilpotent or $\mathcal{A}$
+complete with respect to its augmentation ideal
+\textup{(}\S\ref{sec:i-adic-completion}\textup{)}, and with
+finite-dimensional graded bar pieces whenever the coderived clause
+below is invoked. The bar-cobar counit
 \[
-\psi_0: \Omega_0(\bar{B}_0(\mathcal{A})) \longrightarrow \mathcal{A}
+\psi \colon \Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})) \longrightarrow \mathcal{A}
 \]
-is a quasi-isomorphism of chiral algebras.
-
-If, in addition, $\mathcal{A}$ satisfies axiom~\ref{MK:modular}
-\textup{(}Definition~\ref{def:modular-koszul-chiral}\textup{)}, or lies in the
-standard landscape covered by
-Theorems~\ref{thm:pbw-allgenera-km}--\ref{thm:pbw-allgenera-principal-w},
-then the natural genus-graded map
-\[\psi: \Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})) \longrightarrow \mathcal{A}\]
-induced by the bar-cobar adjunction is a quasi-isomorphism of
-\emph{chiral algebras}: a morphism whose linear component induces an
-isomorphism on cohomology and whose higher components intertwine all
-$A_\infty$ operations up to coherent homotopy
-\textup{(}Remark~\textup{\ref{rem:qi-vs-homology-iso})}.
-
-More precisely:
+is the adjunction counit and hence a canonical morphism of chiral
+algebras. Its strongest proved form is:
 \begin{enumerate}
-\item The map $\psi$ is a morphism of chiral algebras (respects all structure)
+\item \emph{Strict Koszul lane.}
+ If $\mathcal{A}$ is Koszul
+ \textup{(}Definition~\ref{def:koszul-chiral-algebra}; equivalently,
+ $\cA \in \operatorname{Kosz}(X)$\textup{)}, then the genus-$0$
+ counit
+ \[
+ \psi_0 \colon \Omega_0(\bar{B}_0(\mathcal{A})) \longrightarrow \mathcal{A}
+ \]
+ is a quasi-isomorphism of chiral algebras.
+ If, in addition, $\mathcal{A}$ lies on the higher-genus Koszul locus
+ of Theorem~\ref{thm:higher-genus-inversion}
+ \textup{(}for the standard landscape, by
+ Theorems~\ref{thm:pbw-allgenera-km}--\ref{thm:pbw-allgenera-principal-w}\textup{)},
+ then each genus component
+ \[
+ \psi_g \colon \Omega_g(\bar{B}_g(\mathcal{A})) \longrightarrow \mathcal{A}_g
+ \]
+ and the completed series
+ \[
+ \psi = \sum_{g=0}^\infty \hbar^{2g-2}\psi_g
+ \]
+ are quasi-isomorphisms.
 
-\item For each genus $g \geq 1$, the genus-$g$ component:
- \[\psi_g: \Omega_g(\bar{B}_g(\mathcal{A})) \longrightarrow \mathcal{A}_g\]
- is a quasi-isomorphism
+\item \emph{Coderived off-Koszul lane.}
+ For arbitrary complete augmented $\mathcal{A}$ with
+ finite-dimensional graded bar pieces, the factorization counit
+ \[
+ \psi_X \colon \Omega_X \barB_X(\mathcal{A}) \longrightarrow \mathcal{A}
+ \]
+ is a coacyclic-equivalence: its cone
+ \[
+ K_\psi :=
+ \operatorname{cone}\!\bigl(\Omega_X \barB_X(\mathcal{A}) \to \mathcal{A}\bigr)
+ \]
+ is coacyclic. Equivalently, $\psi_X$ is an isomorphism in
+ $D^{\mathrm{co}}(\barB\text{-}\mathrm{CoFact})$, without any
+ Koszulness hypothesis.
 
-\item The full genus-graded map:
- \[\psi = \sum_{g=0}^\infty \hbar^{2g-2}\psi_g: \Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})) \longrightarrow \mathcal{A}\]
- converges and is a quasi-isomorphism in the $\hbar$-adic completion
+\item \emph{Coderived bar-degree filtration.}
+ On the curved cone $K_\psi$, the bar-degree filtration yields the
+ coderived spectral sequence of
+ Proposition~\ref{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}. Its
+ $E_1$ page is computed from the strict associated-graded differential
+ $\operatorname{gr}^0 d$; the curvature relation
+ $\dfib^{\,2} = \kappa(\mathcal{A}) \cdot \omega_g$ is absorbed into
+ positive filtration and does not obstruct the $E_1$ page.
+ On the square-zero Koszul lane this reduces to
+ Theorems~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-spectral-sequence}
+ and~\ref{thm:spectral-sequence-collapse}.
 
-\item There exists a spectral sequence converging to
- $H^\bullet(\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})))$
- with $E_1$-page given by the bar-cobar complex; it collapses at $E_2$
- by the Koszul property (Theorem~\ref{thm:spectral-sequence-collapse})
+\item \emph{Promotion back to ordinary quasi-isomorphism.}
+ If $\kappa(\mathcal{A}) = 0$, or more generally if the coderived
+ bar-degree spectral sequence degenerates to ordinary cohomology on
+ the relevant off-Koszul locus
+ \textup{(}the class~$G/L$ collapse input\textup{)}, then the
+ coderived equivalence of~(2) upgrades to a chain-level
+ quasi-isomorphism. Outside those collapse loci this theorem does not
+ assert an ordinary quasi-isomorphism.
 \end{enumerate}

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

ordinary derived category, and the inversion theorem is a

the curvature forces passage to the coderived setting, and the
