The central result of this chapter is the following.

\subsection{Statement of the main result}

\begin{theorem}[Bar-cobar inversion: strict on the Koszul locus, coderived off it; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}
\textup{[Regime: quadratic on the strict lane; curved-central and
filtered-complete on the coderived lane
\textup{(}cf.\ Convention~\textup{\ref{conv:regime-tags})}.]}

The Heisenberg inversion $\Omega^{\mathrm{ch}}(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\mathcal{H}_k)) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{H}_k$ (\S\ref{sec:frame-inversion}) generalizes to all Koszul chiral algebras.

Let $\mathcal{A}$ be a complete augmented chiral algebra on a
Riemann surface~$X$, with
$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\mathcal{A})$ conilpotent or $\mathcal{A}$
complete with respect to its augmentation ideal
\textup{(}\S\ref{sec:i-adic-completion}\textup{)}, and with
 succeeded in 51ms:

+curvature term lies in positive filtration, so the
+associated-graded $E_1$ page is strict, while coacyclicity of the
+cone records the actual inversion statement. The regime hierarchy
+(Convention~\ref{conv:regime-tags}) organizes these
 four cases.

+$\varepsilon$ is a coacyclic-equivalence and hence an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$.

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

thinking
**Evaluating build issues**

I’m realizing that the slop grep still catches certain comments and unrelated lines in the file. I think I might need a targeted one-pass build since there are no syntax errors. It's possible new labels aren't written because the build didn’t reach that point, but I’m not sure that's necessary. I’m wondering if unresolved references in our new theorem or proposition are causing early stops in the build. There's no fatal issue, but running a script to generate metadata could help clarify. It might be slow, but it could be worth trying.
thinking
**Reviewing metadata generation**

I think running `python3 scripts/generate_metadata.py` might yield a summary, but it could modify metadata, which isn’t ideal. It might rewrite `theorem_registry.md`, leading to unrelated changes that could compromise safety. Maybe I should only run it if necessary. I also want to examine `main.log` for any errors related to my files. I’ll search for “!” lines since the build was successful. Additionally, I should check for unresolved labels, including the new off-koszul theorem and proposition.
thinking
**Considering compilation limitations**

I’m noticing that since the labels aren't being written to the aux file, it might be because the relevant lines are not included. I think I should mention this as a limitation. However, if the user expects verification after each edit, it could be worth attempting a local compile of just the `coderived_models` labels with minimal TeX injection. This might help clarify things without fully redoing the entire compile process.
Plan update
The theorem/proof surfaces in both target files are rewritten and reread. Verification covered AP greps and a fast full build; the build had no fatal TeX error but, as usual on this manuscript, reference convergence remained unstable, including unresolved warnings for the later coderived labels.

  ✓ Diagnose circular references and curved-setting filtration failure; decide exact strongest replacement statements
  ✓ Edit the theorem/proof surfaces in the assigned files and re-read modified sections
  ✓ Run forbidden-pattern greps and the narrowest build/metadata checks; propagate any required status wording in-scope

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+

thinking
**Examining theorem B's requirements**

I realize that theorem B requires a coderived filtration statement, which is interesting. I need to think about incorporating curvature in a way that gets absorbed into positive filtration. This feels a bit tricky, and I want to ensure I'm getting it right. I wonder about the implications of this absorption and how it plays into the broader context of the theorem. There’s a lot to consider!
codex
Rewrote the Theorem B surface in [bar_cobar_adjunction_inversion.tex](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/bar_cobar_adjunction_inversion.tex:1606) and the coderived support surface in [coderived_models.tex](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/coderived_models.tex:243), [coderived_models.tex](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/coderived_models.tex:304), and [coderived_models.tex](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/coderived_models.tex:900). The square-zero spectral sequence is now confined to the strict Koszul lane; the curved/off-locus lane is stated and proved coderived-first via coacyclicity of the counit cone.

Verification: AP greps on the edited files found no bare `\Omega/z` hits and no bar-augmentation/suspension regressions. `make fast` completed without a fatal TeX error, but the global manuscript still did not converge after 4 passes and continued to report large standing undefined-reference churn; the late coderived labels also remained unresolved in that oscillating build, so full build-surface convergence is still open.

## Platonic Rectification Report
`thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi` in [bar_cobar_adjunction_inversion.tex](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/bar_cobar_adjunction_inversion.tex:1606)
- BEFORE: “Bar-cobar inversion is quasi-isomorphism; `ProvedHere`”, with the main theorem stated essentially on the strict/Koszul lane and item `(4)` still functioning like a square-zero spectral-sequence surrogate for the curved setting.
- ISSUE: off-locus coderived inversion was not part of the main theorem surface; the curved lane still leaned on square-zero language and did not isolate the exact extra input needed to recover an ordinary quasi-isomorphism.
- ACTION: STRENGTHENED
- AFTER: the theorem now states the strongest proved package: strict quasi-isomorphism on the Koszul lane, unconditional off-Koszul coderived equivalence via coacyclicity of the counit cone, the coderived bar-degree filtration as the curved replacement for the square-zero spectral sequence, and chain-level promotion only under `\kappa=0` or the class `G/L` collapse input.
- CONFIDENCE: high
- REMAINING GAP: off-locus promotion to ordinary quasi-isomorphism is still conditional outside the collapse loci, by design.

`prop:coderived-adequacy` in [coderived_models.tex](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/coderived_models.tex:243)
- BEFORE: `ProvedHere`, but clause `(a)` was phrased mainly as an isomorphism in the provisional coderived category, with the proof reading like an associated-graded weak-equivalence argument.
- ISSUE: the user-specified gap was that adequacy had to be proved intrinsically in `D^{co}` by showing the counit is a coacyclic-equivalence, independently of the higher-genus inversion theorem.
- ACTION: FILLED
- AFTER: clause `(a)` now proves directly that the cone of the counit is coacyclic, using the Cousin filtration, the strict Chevalley–Cousin counit, and Positselski’s bounded-below filtered criterion; invertibility is now explicitly Verdier-quotient invertibility.
- CONFIDENCE: high
- REMAINING GAP: none on the stated provisional-coderived surface.

`prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence` in [coderived_models.tex](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/coderived_models.tex:304)
- BEFORE: absent.
- ISSUE: there was no explicit curved replacement for the square-zero bar-cobar spectral sequence, so Theorem B had no clean place to send the `d^2=\kappa\omega_g` case.
- ACTION: FILLED
- AFTER: new `ProvedHere` proposition giving the coderived bar-degree filtration criterion: `E_0/E_1` are computed from the strict associated-graded differential, curvature is absorbed into positive filtration, and vanishing of the `E_1` page forces coacyclicity of the cone.
- CONFIDENCE: high
- REMAINING GAP: this proposition controls coderived equivalence; ordinary-abutment upgrade still needs the extra collapse input.

`thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion` in [coderived_models.tex](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/coderived_models.tex:900)
- BEFORE: `ProvedHere`, stated as an isomorphism in the coderived category, with proof phrased through stratumwise coderived isomorphisms and surrounding remarks still advertising the result too loosely.
- ISSUE: the theorem needed to be recast explicitly as a coacyclic-equivalence, and the surrounding prose needed to stop blurring coderived inversion with ordinary quasi-isomorphism.
- ACTION: STRENGTHENED
- AFTER: the theorem now states equivalently that the cone is coacyclic; the proof assembles coacyclicity stratumwise and globally; the surrounding remarks now say the strongest proved package is ordinary quasi-isomorphism on the Koszul lane plus coderived equivalence off it, with no silent promotion.
- CONFIDENCE: high
- REMAINING GAP: the fast global build still did not settle references to this late-file theorem in the oscillating manuscript build.

+The genus-$0$ quasi-isomorphism is proved independently by

 Theorem~\ref{thm:chiral-koszul-duality};
 equivalently, it is the implication
 \ref{ftm:koszul}$\Rightarrow$\ref{ftm:counit} of
 the fundamental theorem of chiral twisting morphisms

-higher-genus inversion package (Theorem~\ref{thm:higher-genus-inversion}).
+Under the higher-genus hypotheses of
+Theorem~\ref{thm:higher-genus-inversion}, the genus-$g$ components
+$\psi_g$ for $g \geq 1$ are quasi-isomorphisms by induction on genus,
+and Theorem~\ref{thm:genus-graded-convergence} supplies the completed
+series statement.
+
+\item \emph{Coderived off-Koszul lane.}
+Theorem~\ref{thm:off-koszul-ran-inversion} proves that the
+factorization counit $\psi_X$ is an isomorphism in the coderived
+category for every complete augmented input with finite-dimensional
+graded bar pieces. Its proof is independent of
+Theorem~\ref{thm:higher-genus-inversion}: it identifies the cone of
+$\psi_X$ as coacyclic by stratified Positselski inversion and the
+Verdier-quotient criterion for
+$D^{\mathrm{co}}(\barB\text{-}\mathrm{CoFact})$.
 
-\item \emph{Full genus-graded convergence and quasi-isomorphism.}
-Under the same higher-genus hypothesis, this is
-Theorem~\ref{thm:genus-graded-convergence}, item~(3), which identifies
-the completed genus series $\psi=\sum_{g\ge 0}\hbar^{2g-2}\psi_g$ and
-proves convergence.
+\item \emph{Coderived bar-degree filtration.}
+On the strict square-zero surface, existence and collapse are exactly
+Theorems~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-spectral-sequence}
+and~\ref{thm:spectral-sequence-collapse}. On the curved surface,
+Proposition~\ref{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+replaces ordinary abutment by coacyclicity of the cone: the
+associated-graded $E_1$ page is strict because the curvature term lies
+in positive filtration.
 
-\item \emph{Spectral sequence statement.}
-Existence and explicit page description are exactly
-Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-spectral-sequence}; collapse for Koszul input is
-Theorem~\ref{thm:spectral-sequence-collapse}.
+\item \emph{Promotion lane.}
+If $\kappa(\mathcal{A}) = 0$, then the curved differential becomes
+square-zero, so Theorem~\ref{thm:conilpotent-reduction} identifies the
+coderived and ordinary derived categories and the coderived
+equivalence of~(D2) is an ordinary quasi-isomorphism. More generally,
+any class~$G/L$ collapse input for the coderived bar-degree spectral
+sequence turns coacyclicity of the cone into acyclicity, hence into an
+ordinary quasi-isomorphism. This is the exact extra hypothesis isolated
+in clause~(4).
 \end{enumerate}
-Combining (D1)--(D4) gives the genus-$0$ clause unconditionally and the
-higher-genus clauses under axiom~\ref{MK:modular}.
+Combining (D1)--(D4) gives the strongest proved package stated in the
+theorem.
 \end{proof}

+\begin{proposition}[Coderived bar-degree spectral sequence;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{prop:coderived-bar-degree-spectral-sequence}
+\index{coderived category!bar-degree spectral sequence|textbf}
+\index{bar-cobar inversion!coderived filtration}
+Let
+$f \colon (M, d_M, \mathcal{F}) \to (N, d_N, \mathcal{F})$
+be a morphism of filtered curved factorization models with
+bounded-below filtration, and let
+$K := \operatorname{cone}(f)$.
+Then the induced filtration on~$K$ yields
+\[
+E_0^{p,q}(K)
+=
+\bigl(F^p K/F^{p+1}K\bigr)^{p+q},
+\qquad
+E_1^{p,q}(K)
+=
+H^{p+q}\bigl(E_0^{p,\bullet}(K), \operatorname{gr}^0 d_K\bigr),
+\]
+where $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ is square-zero because the curvature
+lies in~$F^1$. If $E_1^{p,q}(K)=0$ for all~$p,q$, equivalently if
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ is a quasi-isomorphism of the
+strict associated-graded complexes, then $K$ is coacyclic. Hence $f$
+is an isomorphism in
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$; after applying
+Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}, the same morphism is an
+isomorphism in the full coderived category whenever $M$ and~$N$ lie
+in the essential image of~$\iota$.
+\end{proposition}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The cone inherits a bounded-below filtration from~$M$ and~$N$.
+By Definition~\ref{def:filtered-curved-model}, the curvature term of
+the differential lies in~$F^1$, so the induced differential on
+$E_0(K)$ is $\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K$ and satisfies
+$(\operatorname{gr}^0 d_K)^2 = 0$. This is the strict complex that
+defines the $E_1$ page. Vanishing of $E_1$ is equivalent to
+acyclicity of the associated graded cone
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} K$, or equivalently to
+$\operatorname{gr}_{\mathcal{F}} f$ being a quasi-isomorphism of
+strict complexes.
+
+Positselski's bounded-below filtered criterion
+\cite[Proposition~3.5]{Positselski11} then identifies~$K$ as
+coacyclic. The conclusion that~$f$ becomes an isomorphism in the
+Verdier quotient is exactly the definition of
+$\mathsf{D}^{\mathrm{co}}_{\mathrm{prov}}(X)$. The full coderived
+statement follows from Proposition~\ref{prop:provisional-embedding}.
+\end{proof}
+
